macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Records Fifth Straight Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% last week, marking its first five consecutive weekly declines since 2022, driven by Middle East conflict jitters and rising oil prices to $112 per barrel for Brent crude.

items.title: Middle East Conflict Fuels Energy Supply Shock and Higher Inflation Risks
items.summary: The ongoing Middle East war disrupts oil and gas supplies, pushing governments toward energy security investments while AI-driven power demand accelerates infrastructure spending; central banks face challenges if oil prices persist.

items.title: BIS Warns of Inflation Surge and Growth Slowdown from Middle East War
items.summary: The Bank for International Settlements states the Middle East conflict has disrupted energy supplies, expected to drive inflation up and growth down, urging central banks to monitor inflation expectations closely.

items.title: US 10-Year Treasury Yields Rise to 4.43% as Markets Price Oil Shock
items.summary: Elevated oil prices from Middle East tensions have pushed US 10-year Treasury yields to 4.43%, raising questions on whether central bank rates can keep pace with potential inflation resurgence.

items.title: Economists Anticipate April CPI Report to Show Rising Inflation
items.summary: Marketplace reports economists expect the upcoming April CPI data to indicate inflation pressures building, amid broader cost-of-living increases.

items.title: Retail Investors' AI Chip Stock Shift Signals Potential Boom End
items.summary: A surge in retail investment into AI chip stocks echoes dot-com bubble patterns, potentially signaling the end of the current boom according to investment advisors.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Winds Down Fine-Tuning API Platform
items.summary: OpenAI is discontinuing its fine-tuning API platform, signaling a shift in the company's approach to model customization and deployment strategies.

items.title: Stanford Research Advances LLM Pretraining with Reasoning-Centric Data Integration
items.summary: Stanford CS25 seminar presents breakthrough in large language model pretraining, demonstrating that front-loading reasoning-rich data yields persistent gains in reasoning accuracy that post-training cannot replicate.

items.title: Long Lake Management Completes World's First AI Take-Private Deal
items.summary: Long Lake Management announced its agreement to acquire American Express Global Business Travel for $6.3 billion in what is believed to be the first AI-driven take-private transaction, leveraging its Nexus AI platform for workflow automation.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Citrix Introduces Citrix Platform Flex with Persona-Based Computing
items.summary: Citrix launched Citrix Platform Flex, a full-stack secure access platform that replaces one-size-fits-all licensing with flexible consumption credits based on workforce personas, addressing modern IT pressures with managed desktop services and zero-trust access.

items.title: GIGABYTE Unveils Comprehensive AI Infrastructure Portfolio at COMPUTEX 2026
items.summary: GIGABYTE Technology showcased its end-to-end AI infrastructure portfolio at COMPUTEX 2026 under the theme 'Future Landing,' focusing on deployment, operation, and scaling of AI systems in real-world applications.

items.title: Netmore Announces Smart Gas Metering Solution Collaboration
items.summary: Netmore Group partnered with Green Frog Asset Management and Sensational Systems to deliver end-to-end smart gas metering solutions providing real-time data and improved operational efficiency for utilities.

items.title: InMusic to Acquire Native Instruments
items.summary: InMusic announced its acquisition of Native Instruments, consolidating the music technology industry under one umbrella alongside Moog and M-Audio.

items.title: GM Settles California Lawsuit Over Misuse of Customer Driving Data
items.summary: General Motors agreed to pay $12.75 million to settle a California lawsuit regarding the misuse of customer driving data collected through OnStar and sold to data brokers.
